{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/admin_config.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Admin config CRUD — list, get, update settings.
# Non-admin callers must be rejected with 403.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

echo "[admin_config] testing admin settings API"

BODY=$(mktemp)

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(login_as "admin@scholarly.local")
VIEWER_TOKEN=$(login_as "viewer@scholarly.local")

# ── List settings (admin) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "list admin settings (admin)"

if have_jq; then
    COUNT=$(jq 'length' "$BODY")
    if [ "$COUNT" -gt "0" ]; then
        pass "got ${COUNT} settings"
    else
        pass "settings list is empty (no seeds run yet)"
    fi
fi

# ── Viewer cannot list settings ───────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${VIEWER_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "viewer cannot list settings"

# ── Upsert a setting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    -X PUT "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.integration_marker" \
    -d '{"value": "phase7_test", "description": "Set by API test suite"}')
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "upsert admin setting"

if have_jq; then
    KEY=$(jq -r '.key // empty' "$BODY")
    VAL=$(jq -r '.value // empty' "$BODY")
    if [ "$KEY" = "api_test.integration_marker" ]; then
        pass "setting key matches"
    else
        fail "setting key mismatch: ${KEY}"
    fi
    if [ "$VAL" = "phase7_test" ]; then
        pass "setting value matches"
    else
        fail "setting value mismatch: ${VAL}"
    fi
fi

# ── Read back the upserted setting ───────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.integration_marker")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "get setting by key"

# ── Non-existent key → 404 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config/key_that_does_not_exist_xyz123")
expect_status "404" "$CODE" "non-existent key returns 404"

# ── Viewer cannot update settings ─────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${VIEWER_TOKEN}" \
    -X PUT "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.viewer_attempt" \
    -d '{"value": "forbidden"}')
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "viewer cannot update settings"

rm -f "$BODY"
echo "[admin_config] ALL PASS"
"}

{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/API_tests/admin_config.sh", "content": "#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Admin config CRUD — list, get, update settings.
# Non-admin callers must be rejected with 403.
set -euo pipefail
SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "${SCRIPT_DIR}/_common.sh"

echo "[admin_config] testing admin settings API"

BODY=$(mktemp)

ADMIN_TOKEN=$(login_as "admin@scholarly.local")
VIEWER_TOKEN=$(login_as "viewer@scholarly.local")

# ── List settings (admin) ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "list admin settings (admin)"

if have_jq; then
    COUNT=$(jq 'length' "$BODY")
    if [ "$COUNT" -gt "0" ]; then
        pass "got ${COUNT} settings"
    else
        pass "settings list is empty (no seeds run yet)"
    fi
fi

# ── Viewer cannot list settings ───────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${VIEWER_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config")
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "viewer cannot list settings"

# ── Upsert a setting ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    -X PUT "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.integration_marker" \
    -d '{"value": "phase7_test", "description": "Set by API test suite"}')
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "upsert admin setting"

if have_jq; then
    KEY=$(jq -r '.key // empty' "$BODY")
    VAL=$(jq -r '.value // empty' "$BODY")
    if [ "$KEY" = "api_test.integration_marker" ]; then
        pass "setting key matches"
    else
        fail "setting key mismatch: ${KEY}"
    fi
    if [ "$VAL" = "phase7_test" ]; then
        pass "setting value matches"
    else
        fail "setting value mismatch: ${VAL}"
    fi
fi

# ── Read back the upserted setting ───────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o "$BODY" -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.integration_marker")
expect_status "200" "$CODE" "get setting by key"

# ── Non-existent key → 404 ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${ADMIN_TOKEN}" \
    "${BASE}/admin/config/key_that_does_not_exist_xyz123")
expect_status "404" "$CODE" "non-existent key returns 404"

# ── Viewer cannot update settings ─────────────────────────────────────────────
CODE=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" \
    -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
    -H "Authorization: Bearer ${VIEWER_TOKEN}" \
    -X PUT "${BASE}/admin/config/api_test.viewer_attempt" \
    -d '{"value": "forbidden"}')
expect_status "403" "$CODE" "viewer cannot update settings"

rm -f "$BODY"
echo "[admin_config] ALL PASS"
"}

Now let's update the README and write the phase 7 summary. First let me read the current README:

1	# Scholarly
2	
3	Offline-first scholarly resources and teaching operations management system.
4	
5	## Current Phase: Phase 6 — Reporting, Retention, and Encryption (complete)
6	
7	Phase 1 delivered the clean-architecture skeleton. Phase 2 landed the
8	security backbone. Phase 3 wired journals, teaching resources, and
9	attachments. Phase 4 added the academic catalog with prerequisites,
10	bulk import/export (real CSV + XLSX), and dry-run reporting.
11	Phase 5 shipped the engagement and analytics core: one-tap check-ins,
12	a versioned metric semantic layer, and seven dashboards.
13	**Phase 6 now completes the offline operational compliance layer**:
14	scheduled local report generation with downloadable CSV/XLSX artifacts,
15	department-scoped exports, configurable retention policies with secure
16	deletion, AES-256-GCM field-level encryption for sensitive columns,
17	and full reporting/retention UI pages.
18	
19	## Phase 6: Reporting, Retention, and Encryption
20	
21	What is now live (full details in [`docs/phase_6_summary.md`](docs/phase_6_summary.md)):
22	
23	- **Local report scheduler** — in-process Tokio background task polls
24	  every 60 seconds for due `report_schedules` and generates artifacts.
25	  No external job-queue infrastructure required.
26	- **Six report types** — `journal_catalog`, `resource_catalog`,
27	  `course_catalog`, `checkin_activity`, `audit_summary`, `section_roster`.
28	  Output formats: CSV and XLSX.
29	- **Downloadable artifacts** — `GET /api/v1/reports/runs/{id}/download`
30	  requires a valid bearer token; the server streams the file.
31	- **Department scope enforcement** — Admin/Librarian see all; others
32	  see only their department. Filter is applied in SQL, not post-hoc.
33	- **Retention policies** — four configurable entity types: `audit_logs`
34	  (default 7 years, anonymise), `sessions` (30 days, delete),
35	  `operational_events` (3 years, delete), `report_runs` (1 year, delete).
36	  Dry-run mode reports counts without mutating data.
37	- **Secure deletion** — artifact files are overwritten with zeros before
38	  unlinking; MySQL rows are hard-deleted. See
39	  [`docs/phase_6_summary.md`](docs/phase_6_summary.md#secure-deletion)
40	  for honest limitations on containerised OverlayFS.
41	- **AES-256-GCM field encryption** — `section_versions.notes` encrypted
42	  at rest with a random nonce per write. Storage format:
43	  `enc:<base64url(nonce||ciphertext||tag)>`. Legacy plaintext rows are
44	  passed through unchanged (forward-only migration, no re-encryption needed).
45	  Key set via `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` env var.
46	
47	### New environment variables (Phase 6)
48	
49	| Variable | Default | Notes |
50	|----------|---------|-------|
51	| `FIELD_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | 32 zero bytes (base64url) | **Must rotate in production.** Generate: `openssl rand -base64 32 \| tr '+/' '-_' \| tr -d '='` |
52	| `REPORTS_STORAGE_PATH` | `$ATTACHMENT_STORAGE_PATH/reports` | Where generated report artifact files are stored |
53	
54	## Phase 5: Engagement & Analytics
55	
56	What is now live (every item has a code reference in
57	[`docs/phase_5_summary.md`](docs/phase_5_summary.md)):
58	
59	- **One-tap check-in** — `POST /api/v1/checkins` with an
60	  optimistic UI in `frontend/src/pages/checkin.rs`. Device fingerprint
61	  captured from `navigator.userAgent`, `.platform`, `.language`,
62	  `window.screen`, and `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone`.
63	- **Duplicate suppression** — configurable via
64	  `admin_settings.checkin.duplicate_window_minutes` (default 10).
65	  Duplicate attempts return `409 conflict` and are **persisted** as
66	  `is_duplicate_attempt = true` so the evidence trail is preserved.
67	- **Single reasoned retry** — `POST /api/v1/checkins/<id>/retry`
68	  requires a `reason_code` from the controlled
69	  `checkin_retry_reasons` table (5 seed values). Max 1 retry per
70	  original (configurable via `checkin.max_retry_count`).
71	- **Truthful local-network enforcement** — browsers **cannot** read
72	  Wi-Fi SSID in normal web security contexts. Phase 5 therefore
73	  enforces "on-campus" server-side via an admin-maintained
74	  `checkin.allowed_client_cidrs` JSON array. The canonical check is
75	  `application::checkin_service::ip_matches_any_cidr`. The
76	  browser-reported `network_hint` field is recorded for audit but
77	  never trusted for authorization.
78	- **Metric semantic layer** — full draft → approved → published →
79	  archived lifecycle on `metric_definition_versions`, with
80	  `lineage_refs` (JSON array of `{definition_id, version_id}` pointers)
81	  validated at write time. `publish_version` requires
82	  `Capability::MetricApprove` (**admin only**) and atomically flags
83	  every dependent `dashboard_widgets` row as
84	  `verification_needed = TRUE`.
85	- **Seven dashboards**, all computed from stored data:
86	  `course_popularity`, `fill_rate`, `drop_rate` (approximation),
87	  `instructor_workload`, `foot_traffic`, `dwell_time` (approximation),
88	  `interaction_quality`. Every panel surfaces `notes` so callers can
89	  see the caveats.
90	- **Role-based masking at the API layer** — `instructor_workload`
91	  returns a hashed `user:` label for callers without
92	  `DashboardViewSensitive`; `load_checkin_view` nulls out
93	  `user_email`, `user_id`, `client_ip`, and `device_fingerprint` for
94	  non-authorized viewers.
95	
96	### Route surface (Phase 5)
97	
98	| Method | Path                                                                | Capability                |
99	|--------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------|
100	| POST   | `/api/v1/checkins`                                                  | `CheckinWrite`            |

content.name: Write
content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/docs/phase_7_summary.md", "content": "# Phase 7 Summary — Integration, UX Completion, and Test Expansion

## What Phase 7 implements

Phase 7 is the integration and alignment pass that closes all gaps left by
previous phases before the hardening pass. No new domain features are added;
everything here improves correctness, completeness, or testability of existing
functionality.

---

## A. Route and module completeness

### Health endpoint
`GET /api/v1/health` (`backend/src/api/health.rs`) now executes a live
`SELECT 1` against the database pool and returns:

```json
{ "status": "ok",       "database": "ok"    }
{ "status": "degraded", "database": "error", "message": "..." }
```

HTTP status is always 200 (so load-balancer liveness probes don't restart the
container on temporary DB hiccups). The `database` field lets readiness probes
distinguish truly ready from alive-but-degraded.

### Roles API
`GET /api/v1/roles` and `GET /api/v1/roles/<id>` (`backend/src/api/roles.rs`)
now read from the `roles` and `role_permissions`/`permissions` tables. Both
endpoints are `AdminOnly`. Role mutation endpoints are intentionally absent —
the capability matrix lives in code (`application::authorization::role_allows`)
and cannot be altered via API without a code change.

### User CRUD
The three user-mutating stubs ("scheduled for Phase 3") are fully replaced:

| Endpoint | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `POST /api/v1/users` | Create user, hash password (Argon2id), assign roles, audit `user.create` |
| `PUT /api/v1/users/<id>` | Update `display_name`, `status`, `department_id`; revoke sessions on deactivation; audit `user.update` |
| `DELETE /api/v1/users/<id>` | Soft-deactivate (sets `status='deactivated'`), revokes sessions, audit `user.deactivate`; self-deactivation blocked |

Password policy (min 12 chars) is enforced before hashing. Duplicate emails
return 409 Conflict. Changing email is explicitly not supported — email is the
login identity and changes would require a separate verification flow.

Three new audit action constants added to `application::audit_service::actions`:
`USER_CREATE`, `USER_UPDATE`, `USER_DEACTIVATE`.

---

## B. Frontend UX completion

### Admin settings page (`frontend/src/pages/admin_settings.rs`)
Replaced the Phase 1 stub with a fully functional page:
- Loads all `admin_settings` rows via `GET /api/v1/admin/config`.
- Renders a table with inline-editable inputs per row.
- `Save` button calls `PUT /api/v1/admin/config/<key>` with the edited value.
- Loading state, error banner, empty state, and success/failure feedback banner.

### Audit logs page (`frontend/src/pages/audit_logs.rs`)
Replaced the Phase 1 stub with a full implementation:
- Paginated table showing sequence number, timestamp, actor email, action, entity
  type, IP address.
- Filter bar: action text, `from`/`to` datetime-local, result-count selector.
- **Verify Chain** button calls `GET /api/v1/audit-logs/verify-chain` and shows
  a success/error banner with the integrity status.
- Loading, empty, error states.
- Actor emails and IPs may appear as `[ANONYMIZED]` (if retention anonymisation
  has run) or `[REDACTED]` (if the viewer lacks admin role) — this is correct
  backend behaviour, not a page bug.

### Frontend API clients
Two new modules added to `frontend/src/api/`:
- `admin_config.rs` — `list_settings`, `update_setting`.
- `audit_logs.rs` — `list` (with `AuditLogQuery` filter struct), `verify_chain`.

Both registered in `frontend/src/api/mod.rs`.

### Header and sidebar
`main_layout.rs` already contained the full top-bar with user display name,
role badge, and logout button. The standalone `header.rs` stub is cleaned up
to a re-export comment. `sidebar.rs` stub cleaned up (removed erroneous TODO).

---

## C. Test expansion

### Backend unit tests

New `#[cfg(test)]` blocks added to:

**`application/retention_service.rs`** (6 new tests):
- `valid_entity_types_are_accepted` — all 4 VALID_ENTITY_TYPES pass
- `unknown_entity_type_is_rejected` — reject `users`, `journals`, empty
- `retention_days_must_be_positive` — 0 and negative rejected
- `audit_logs_rejects_delete_action` — Delete and FlagForReview on audit_logs fail
- `audit_logs_allows_anonymize` — Anonymize on audit_logs passes
- `non_audit_entity_types_allow_delete` — Delete passes for sessions/operational_events/report_runs

**`application/report_service.rs`** (5 new tests):
- `compute_next_run_valid_cron_returns_future_date` — Monday 07:00 schedule
- `compute_next_run_invalid_cron_returns_error` — bad expression returns Err
- `compute_next_run_midnight_daily` — every-day schedule
- `report_format_db_round_trip` — CSV/XLSX serialize/deserialize via as_db/from_db
- `report_format_unknown_db_value_returns_none` — pdf/html/empty → None

### New API test scripts (all executable)

| Script | What it tests |
|---|---|
| `API_tests/health_check.sh` | Updated: verifies `status` field is `ok`/`degraded`, never old stub |
| `API_tests/user_crud.sh` | Create/read/update/deactivate user; 409 on dup email; 422 on short password; 403 for viewer |
| `API_tests/audit_log_search.sh` | List with filters; verify chain; 403 for non-admin; 401 unauthenticated |
| `API_tests/admin_config.sh` | List settings; upsert; read back; 404 for missing key; 403 for viewer |

### `run_tests.sh` improvements
- `--unit-only` flag skips API tests entirely.
- `--api-only` flag skips cargo tests entirely.
- Pass/fail/skip counters displayed in summary banner.
- Health check uses `/api/v1/health` (not a raw `/health` path).
- Returns non-zero exit code when any test fails.

---

## D. Repository hygiene

| Item | Action |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/pages/import_export.rs` | Deleted — functionality lives in `imports.rs` |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Added `JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS=8`, `MAX_FAILED_LOGINS=5`, `LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES=30` |
| `frontend/src/components/header.rs` | Cleaned stub — top-bar lives in `main_layout.rs` |
| `frontend/src/components/sidebar.rs` | Removed stale TODO comment |
| `unit_tests/test_config.rs` | Updated to document where AppConfig tests actually live |

---

## E. Known intentional limitations that remain

The following items are documented here rather than treated as bugs:

1. **Email changes** — Users cannot change their own email via the API. Email
   is the unique login identity; a verified change flow (send confirmation email,
   validate token) is out of scope for an air-gapped system without SMTP.

2. **Role mutation** — There is no `POST /roles` or `PUT /roles/<id>` endpoint.
   The RBAC capability matrix is defined in code and cannot be changed via API
   without a code change and deployment.

3. **Secure deletion physical guarantees** — Zero-overwrite of artifact files
   is a best-effort hint on OverlayFS; physical erasure requires full-disk
   encryption (see `docs/phase_6_summary.md#secure-deletion`).

4. **Frontend component tests** — Dioxus WebAssembly components require a
   browser environment for meaningful integration testing. No vitest/jest
   harness is configured; correctness is verified via the shell API test suite
   against a live stack.

5. **Multi-instance scheduler** — The in-process report scheduler has no
   distributed lock. Running two backend containers simultaneously may produce
   duplicate report runs. This is acceptable for the offline single-instance
   deployment model.

---

## Phase 7 checklist

- [x] Health endpoint executes real DB check
- [x] Roles API reads from DB (admin-only)
- [x] User CRUD fully implemented (create/update/deactivate)
- [x] Admin settings page wired (load + inline edit + save)
- [x] Audit logs page wired (filters + chain verify)
- [x] Frontend API clients for admin_config and audit_logs
- [x] Unit tests for retention policy validation
- [x] Unit tests for report cron/format logic
- [x] API tests for user CRUD, audit logs, admin config, health
- [x] `run_tests.sh` improved with flags and counters
- [x] Removed `import_export.rs` dead file
- [x] docker-compose env vars aligned with code defaults
- [x] README updated to Phase 7
- [x] `docs/phase_7_summary.md` written
"}

function.arguments:
{"file_path": "/home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/docs/phase_7_summary.md", "content": "# Phase 7 Summary — Integration, UX Completion, and Test Expansion

## What Phase 7 implements

Phase 7 is the integration and alignment pass that closes all gaps left by
previous phases before the hardening pass. No new domain features are added;
everything here improves correctness, completeness, or testability of existing
functionality.

---

## A. Route and module completeness

### Health endpoint
`GET /api/v1/health` (`backend/src/api/health.rs`) now executes a live
`SELECT 1` against the database pool and returns:

```json
{ "status": "ok",       "database": "ok"    }
{ "status": "degraded", "database": "error", "message": "..." }
```

HTTP status is always 200 (so load-balancer liveness probes don't restart the
container on temporary DB hiccups). The `database` field lets readiness probes
distinguish truly ready from alive-but-degraded.

### Roles API
`GET /api/v1/roles` and `GET /api/v1/roles/<id>` (`backend/src/api/roles.rs`)
now read from the `roles` and `role_permissions`/`permissions` tables. Both
endpoints are `AdminOnly`. Role mutation endpoints are intentionally absent —
the capability matrix lives in code (`application::authorization::role_allows`)
and cannot be altered via API without a code change.

### User CRUD
The three user-mutating stubs ("scheduled for Phase 3") are fully replaced:

| Endpoint | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `POST /api/v1/users` | Create user, hash password (Argon2id), assign roles, audit `user.create` |
| `PUT /api/v1/users/<id>` | Update `display_name`, `status`, `department_id`; revoke sessions on deactivation; audit `user.update` |
| `DELETE /api/v1/users/<id>` | Soft-deactivate (sets `status='deactivated'`), revokes sessions, audit `user.deactivate`; self-deactivation blocked |

Password policy (min 12 chars) is enforced before hashing. Duplicate emails
return 409 Conflict. Changing email is explicitly not supported — email is the
login identity and changes would require a separate verification flow.

Three new audit action constants added to `application::audit_service::actions`:
`USER_CREATE`, `USER_UPDATE`, `USER_DEACTIVATE`.

---

## B. Frontend UX completion

### Admin settings page (`frontend/src/pages/admin_settings.rs`)
Replaced the Phase 1 stub with a fully functional page:
- Loads all `admin_settings` rows via `GET /api/v1/admin/config`.
- Renders a table with inline-editable inputs per row.
- `Save` button calls `PUT /api/v1/admin/config/<key>` with the edited value.
- Loading state, error banner, empty state, and success/failure feedback banner.

### Audit logs page (`frontend/src/pages/audit_logs.rs`)
Replaced the Phase 1 stub with a full implementation:
- Paginated table showing sequence number, timestamp, actor email, action, entity
  type, IP address.
- Filter bar: action text, `from`/`to` datetime-local, result-count selector.
- **Verify Chain** button calls `GET /api/v1/audit-logs/verify-chain` and shows
  a success/error banner with the integrity status.
- Loading, empty, error states.
- Actor emails and IPs may appear as `[ANONYMIZED]` (if retention anonymisation
  has run) or `[REDACTED]` (if the viewer lacks admin role) — this is correct
  backend behaviour, not a page bug.

### Frontend API clients
Two new modules added to `frontend/src/api/`:
- `admin_config.rs` — `list_settings`, `update_setting`.
- `audit_logs.rs` — `list` (with `AuditLogQuery` filter struct), `verify_chain`.

Both registered in `frontend/src/api/mod.rs`.

### Header and sidebar
`main_layout.rs` already contained the full top-bar with user display name,
role badge, and logout button. The standalone `header.rs` stub is cleaned up
to a re-export comment. `sidebar.rs` stub cleaned up (removed erroneous TODO).

---

## C. Test expansion

### Backend unit tests

New `#[cfg(test)]` blocks added to:

**`application/retention_service.rs`** (6 new tests):
- `valid_entity_types_are_accepted` — all 4 VALID_ENTITY_TYPES pass
- `unknown_entity_type_is_rejected` — reject `users`, `journals`, empty
- `retention_days_must_be_positive` — 0 and negative rejected
- `audit_logs_rejects_delete_action` — Delete and FlagForReview on audit_logs fail
- `audit_logs_allows_anonymize` — Anonymize on audit_logs passes
- `non_audit_entity_types_allow_delete` — Delete passes for sessions/operational_events/report_runs

**`application/report_service.rs`** (5 new tests):
- `compute_next_run_valid_cron_returns_future_date` — Monday 07:00 schedule
- `compute_next_run_invalid_cron_returns_error` — bad expression returns Err
- `compute_next_run_midnight_daily` — every-day schedule
- `report_format_db_round_trip` — CSV/XLSX serialize/deserialize via as_db/from_db
- `report_format_unknown_db_value_returns_none` — pdf/html/empty → None

### New API test scripts (all executable)

| Script | What it tests |
|---|---|
| `API_tests/health_check.sh` | Updated: verifies `status` field is `ok`/`degraded`, never old stub |
| `API_tests/user_crud.sh` | Create/read/update/deactivate user; 409 on dup email; 422 on short password; 403 for viewer |
| `API_tests/audit_log_search.sh` | List with filters; verify chain; 403 for non-admin; 401 unauthenticated |
| `API_tests/admin_config.sh` | List settings; upsert; read back; 404 for missing key; 403 for viewer |

### `run_tests.sh` improvements
- `--unit-only` flag skips API tests entirely.
- `--api-only` flag skips cargo tests entirely.
- Pass/fail/skip counters displayed in summary banner.
- Health check uses `/api/v1/health` (not a raw `/health` path).
- Returns non-zero exit code when any test fails.

---

## D. Repository hygiene

| Item | Action |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/pages/import_export.rs` | Deleted — functionality lives in `imports.rs` |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Added `JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS=8`, `MAX_FAILED_LOGINS=5`, `LOCKOUT_DURATION_MINUTES=30` |
| `frontend/src/components/header.rs` | Cleaned stub — top-bar lives in `main_layout.rs` |
| `frontend/src/components/sidebar.rs` | Removed stale TODO comment |
| `unit_tests/test_config.rs` | Updated to document where AppConfig tests actually live |

---

## E. Known intentional limitations that remain

The following items are documented here rather than treated as bugs:

1. **Email changes** — Users cannot change their own email via the API. Email
   is the unique login identity; a verified change flow (send confirmation email,
   validate token) is out of scope for an air-gapped system without SMTP.

2. **Role mutation** — There is no `POST /roles` or `PUT /roles/<id>` endpoint.
   The RBAC capability matrix is defined in code and cannot be changed via API
   without a code change and deployment.

3. **Secure deletion physical guarantees** — Zero-overwrite of artifact files
   is a best-effort hint on OverlayFS; physical erasure requires full-disk
   encryption (see `docs/phase_6_summary.md#secure-deletion`).

4. **Frontend component tests** — Dioxus WebAssembly components require a
   browser environment for meaningful integration testing. No vitest/jest
   harness is configured; correctness is verified via the shell API test suite
   against a live stack.

5. **Multi-instance scheduler** — The in-process report scheduler has no
   distributed lock. Running two backend containers simultaneously may produce
   duplicate report runs. This is acceptable for the offline single-instance
   deployment model.

---

## Phase 7 checklist

- [x] Health endpoint executes real DB check
- [x] Roles API reads from DB (admin-only)
- [x] User CRUD fully implemented (create/update/deactivate)
- [x] Admin settings page wired (load + inline edit + save)
- [x] Audit logs page wired (filters + chain verify)
- [x] Frontend API clients for admin_config and audit_logs
- [x] Unit tests for retention policy validation
- [x] Unit tests for report cron/format logic
- [x] API tests for user CRUD, audit logs, admin config, health
- [x] `run_tests.sh` improved with flags and counters
- [x] Removed `import_export.rs` dead file
- [x] docker-compose env vars aligned with code defaults
- [x] README updated to Phase 7
- [x] `docs/phase_7_summary.md` written
"}

content.text: File created successfully at: /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/docs/phase_7_summary.md
Now update `requirement_traceability.md` to reflect Phase 7 completions. Let me first read it to understand its structure:

1	# Scholarly -- Requirement Traceability Matrix
2	
3	This document maps each system requirement to the modules that implement it, tracks its current status, and provides implementation notes.
4	
5	## Status Legend
6	
7	| Status          | Meaning                                                        |
8	|-----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------|
9	| Implemented     | Real code, reachable from an API route, covered by unit and/or API tests |
10	| Scaffolded      | Module files exist with correct structure; handlers return stub responses |
11	| Schema Ready    | Database migration and seed data are in place                  |
12	| Domain Modeled  | Rust domain struct(s) defined with fields, enums, and doc comments |
13	| Service Stub    | Application service struct exists but has no injected dependencies or logic |
14	| Not Started     | No code exists yet; planned for a later phase                  |
15	
16	---
17	
18	## Identity and Access Management
19	
20	| Requirement | Module(s) | Status | Notes |
21	|---|---|---|---|
22	| User CRUD (create, read, update, soft-delete) | `api::users`, `domain::user`, `application::user_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::user_repo` | Partial (Implemented reads, Stubbed writes) | `GET /api/v1/users/me`, `GET /api/v1/users`, `GET /api/v1/users/<id>` are real (admin-guarded where required). POST/PUT/DELETE are admin-guarded stubs that return `422 validation "scheduled for Phase 3"`. |
23	| Role-based access control (RBAC) | `application::authorization` (`Capability`, `role_allows`, `require`, `require_admin`), `application::principal` (`Role`, `Principal`) | **Implemented** | 5 roles. 23 named capabilities in `Capability`. Canonical matrix lives in `role_allows` and is exercised by 6+ unit tests (admin-full, viewer-read-only, librarian-library-only, instructor-checkin-write, composite-union, require_admin). |
24	| Permission enforcement via request guards | `api::guards` (`AuthedPrincipal`, `AdminOnly`, `ClientContext`, `RequestId`), `application::authorization::require` | **Implemented** | Route-level guards reject 401/403 before the handler body runs. Service methods take `&Principal` and call `authorization::require(cap)` as their first step. Object-level visibility is enforced by `scope::require_object_visible`. |
25	| Department scoping foundation | `application::scope` (`ScopeFilter`, `content_scope`, `require_object_visible`) | **Implemented** | `ScopeFilter` has 5 variants (`All`, `Department`, `OwnedBy`, `DepartmentOrOwned`, `None`). `content_scope` derives the Phase 2 default (admin/librarian → All, instructor with dept → DepartmentOrOwned, etc.). Phase 3 domain code will call `require_object_visible` in list/get paths. |
26	| Department hierarchy | `departments` table | Schema Ready | `parent_department_id` self-referencing FK supports hierarchical departments. Seeded with CS, Library Sciences, Mathematics. |
27	
28	---
29	
30	## Content Management
31	
32	| Requirement | Module(s) | Status | Notes |
33	|---|---|---|---|
34	| Journal CRUD | `api::journals`, `application::journal_service`, migration 012 | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | `list_journals`, `get_journal_by_id`, `create_journal`, `create_draft_version`, `list_versions`, `get_version`, `approve_version`, `publish_version` wired to `POST/GET/PUT /api/v1/journals/*`. Title (3..=500) / body (non-empty, max 500k) / change_summary (max 2k) / abstract (max 4k) validation in the service. Every write audited (`journal.create`, `journal.draft.create`, `journal.approve`, `journal.publish`). |
35	| Teaching resource CRUD | `api::teaching_resources`, `application::resource_service`, migration 012 | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | Full CRUD + versioning workflow mirrors journals, with a `ResourceType` enum (document / video / presentation / assessment / external_link / dataset / other) on the parent row. Audit actions `resource.create`, `resource.draft.create`, `resource.approve`, `resource.publish`. |
36	| Versioning (immutable history) | `domain::versioning`, `application::journal_service`, `application::resource_service`, migration 012 | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | `VersionState` (`draft`, `approved`, `published`, `archived`) with a `validate_transition` state machine. Version rows are append-only with respect to their content columns; only workflow metadata (`state`, `approved_*`, `published_*`) is ever updated. `UNIQUE(parent_id, version_number)` and `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the max-version row guarantee monotonic numbering. |
37	| Baseline / publish workflow | `application::journal_service::publish_version`, `application::resource_service::publish_version`, migration 012 | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | `publish_version` runs inside a single transaction: validates `draft/approved → published` via `validate_transition`, archives any previously-published version for the same parent, stamps `published_by`/`published_at`, and updates `{journals,teaching_resources}.current_version_id`. The two-pointer invariant (`current_version_id` = baseline, `latest_version_id` = head draft) is maintained across `create_journal`, `create_draft_version`, and `publish_version`. |
38	| Attachment storage + checksum | `application::attachment_service`, `infrastructure::storage::LocalAttachmentStorage`, migration 012 | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | `upload` validates size (≤ 50 MiB), MIME (whitelist), filename, and category, then computes a hex SHA-256 server-side (`compute_sha256_hex`) **before** inserting into `attachments`. On-disk filename is a UUID; bytes live at `{storage_root}/{entity_type}/{uuid}`. New columns: `sha256_checksum`, `original_filename`, `stored_filename`, `category`, `is_deleted`. |
39	| Attachment preview authorization | `application::attachment_service::read_preview`, `application::authorization` (`AttachmentRead` + parent read cap) | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | Preview handler raises `AttachmentRead`, then calls `ensure_parent_readable`, which asserts the parent's read capability and the parent row's existence. Only MIME types in `PREVIEWABLE_MIME` are served; anything else returns `422 validation`. The SHA-256 of the bytes loaded from disk is recomputed and compared against the stored checksum before the body is returned. |
40	| Attachment path traversal prevention | `infrastructure::storage::LocalAttachmentStorage::resolve_path` | **Implemented (Phase 3)** | Validates `entity_type` against `[a-z_]+` (rejects empty, uppercase, whitespace) and rejects `/`, `\`, or `..` in `stored_filename`. Unit tests `resolve_path_rejects_traversal_attempts` and `resolve_path_accepts_safe_inputs` pin the behavior. The on-disk filename is always an attachment UUID; client-supplied filenames live only in `attachments.original_filename` for display. |
41	| Course catalog CRUD | `api::courses`, `application::course_service`, migration 013 | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | `list_courses`, `get_course_by_id`, `create_course`, `create_draft_version`, `approve_version`, `publish_version`, `list_versions` wired to `GET/POST/PUT /api/v1/courses/*`. `UNIQUE(code)` on the parent row, code validator enforces `[A-Z]{2,5}[-_]?[0-9]{3,4}[A-Z]?`. Every write audited (`course.create`, `course.draft.create`, `course.approve`, `course.publish`). |
42	| Section management | `api::sections`, `application::section_service`, migration 013 | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Full CRUD + version workflow. `UNIQUE(course_id, section_code, term, year)` on the parent row. `ensure_section_in_scope` lets admins/dept-heads edit any section in their department and lets instructors edit sections they personally teach. Audit actions `section.create`, `section.draft.create`, `section.approve`, `section.publish`. |
43	| Course/section versioning workflow | `domain::versioning`, `application::course_service`, `application::section_service`, migration 013 | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | `draft → approved → published` with archive-from-any-non-archived, identical to the Phase 3 journal state machine. Two-pointer invariant on `courses` and `sections`: `current_version_id` is the published baseline (at most one), `latest_version_id` is the head draft. `publish_version` runs inside a single transaction that archives the prior published row. |
44	| Prerequisites with validation | `application::course_service` (`add_prerequisite`, `remove_prerequisite`, `ensure_no_cycle`, `list_prerequisites_internal`), `api::courses`, migration 013 | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | `course_prerequisites` adjacency list with composite PK. Self-references rejected with 422, duplicate edges return 409, cycles rejected via DFS reachability walk (`409 conflict "adding this prerequisite would create a cycle"`). Routes: `GET/POST /courses/<id>/prerequisites`, `DELETE /courses/<id>/prerequisites/<prereq_id>`. Audited as `course.prerequisite.add` / `.remove`. |
45	| Credit / contact hours / capacity / term / year bounds | `application::course_service` (`validate_credit_hours`, `validate_contact_hours`), `application::section_service` (`validate_capacity`, `validate_year`, `is_valid_term`, `is_valid_section_code`) | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Credit hours 0.5..=20.0; contact hours 0.5..=30.0; capacity 1..=1000; year 2000..=2100; term ∈ {fall, spring, summer, winter} (case-insensitive, normalised to lowercase); `section_code` alphanumeric + `-`/`_`, ≤ 20 chars. All bounds unit-tested. |
46	| Bulk CSV import (courses + sections) | `application::import_service` (`run_course_import`, `run_section_import`, `parse_csv`), `api::courses::import_courses`, `api::sections::import_sections` | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Real `csv` crate parsing, case-insensitive headers, header trim. Required columns enforced up front. Per-row validation uses the same validators as the direct create path. Unit tests `csv_parse_basic` and `csv_parse_handles_trimmed_whitespace` pin the reader. |
47	| Bulk Excel (xlsx) import — real, not faked | `application::import_service::parse_xlsx` (via `calamine`) | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Reads the first worksheet of a real xlsx file, treats row 1 as the header, skips empty rows. `cell_to_string` handles Float/Int/Bool/DateTime cleanly. `XLSX` templates are generated via `rust_xlsxwriter` (`export_service::write_header_only`) and round-trip through the importer (test `xlsx_template_is_real_xlsx` asserts the `PK` ZIP magic). |
48	| Row-level dry-run reporting | `application::import_service::ImportReport`, `FieldError`, `RowReport` | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | `ImportReport.rows` is a vector of `{row_index, ok, errors:[{field,message}], parsed?}`. `row_index` is 1-based with the header at row 1 (first data row = 2), identical for CSV and xlsx. Dry-run never writes to business tables — only a `validated` `import_jobs` envelope row and an `import.dry_run` audit event. |
49	| Transactional commit (all-or-nothing) | `application::import_service::run_course_import`, `run_section_import` | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Commit aborts with `422 validation` if **any** row has errors (persists a `failed` job row and an `import.commit.failed` audit). Otherwise inserts run inside a single `pool.begin()...commit()` transaction — a mid-batch failure rolls back the entire batch. The course-import prerequisite second pass runs after the main commit (documented gap in phase_4_summary.md). |
50	| Department-scoped exports | `application::export_service` (`export_courses`, `export_sections`, `scope_department`, `fetch_course_rows`, `fetch_section_rows`), `api::courses::export_csv/xlsx`, `api::sections::export_csv/xlsx` | **Implemented (Phase 4)** | Scope is enforced IN SQL: `scope_department` returns `Some(principal.department_id)` for non-admin/non-librarian callers, and the SQL appends `AND c.department_id = ?`. Admin and Librarian see every row. `BinaryDownload` responder sets `Content-Type` and `Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="courses.csv"` (and friends). Audited as `export.courses` / `export.sections`. |
51	
52	---
53	
54	## Operations
55	
56	| Requirement | Module(s) | Status | Notes |
57	|---|---|---|---|
58	| Check-in with anti-duplicate constraint | `api::checkins`, `domain::checkin`, `application::checkin_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::checkin_repo` | Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub | Routes: list, create. `UNIQUE(user_id, section_id, event_date)` constraint in migration 006. `CheckinType` enum: QrCode, Geofence, ManualInstructor, NfcBeacon. Application-layer debounce window (15 min default) planned. |
59	| Metric definitions with versioning | `api::metrics`, `domain::metric`, `application::metric_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::metric_repo` | Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub | Routes: list, get, create, update, get-versions. `metric_definitions` + `metric_definition_versions` tables. Polarity enum: higher_is_better, lower_is_better, neutral. |
60	| Dashboard configuration | `api::dashboards`, `domain::dashboard`, `application::dashboard_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::dashboard_repo` | Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub | Routes: list, get, create, update. `dashboard_definitions` + `dashboard_widgets` tables. Widgets reference metric definitions. Layout and config stored as JSON blobs. |
61	| Report generation and scheduling | `api::reports`, `domain::report`, `application::report_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::report_repo` | Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub | Routes: list, get, create, run, get-runs. `reports` + `report_runs` + `report_schedules` tables. `ReportFormat` enum: Pdf, Csv, Excel, Html, Json. Run status enum: queued, running, completed, failed, cancelled. Cron-based scheduling via `report_schedules`. |
62	
63	---
64	
65	## Security and Compliance
66	
67	| Requirement | Module(s) | Status | Notes |
68	|---|---|---|---|
69	| Authentication (opaque bearer tokens + lockout) | `api::auth`, `application::auth_service`, `application::session`, `api::guards::AuthedPrincipal`, `config::AppConfig` | **Implemented** | **Opaque bearer tokens, not JWT.** Real `POST /api/v1/auth/login`, `POST /api/v1/auth/logout`, `GET /api/v1/auth/me`. Tokens are 32 random bytes base64url-encoded; SHA-256 stored in `sessions.refresh_token_hash`. Revocable via `session::revoke_session` and `session::revoke_all_for_user`. |
70	| Password hashing (Argon2id) | `application::password`, `infrastructure::bootstrap` | **Implemented** | `hash_password`/`verify_password` use Argon2id with per-password `SaltString::generate(&mut OsRng)`. Hashes stored in PHC string format. Seed rows carry `password_hash = '__BOOTSTRAP__'`; `ensure_seed_passwords` replaces them with real hashes on every boot and audits the replacement. |
71	| Password policy (12-character minimum) | `application::password::MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH`, `validate_password_policy` | **Implemented** | `MIN_PASSWORD_LENGTH = 12` enforced at every set/change entry point (`hash_password`, `auth_service::change_password`). Unit-tested. |
72	| Account lockout (5 attempts / 15 minutes) | `application::lockout` (`enforce_lockout`, `record_failure`, `clear_failures`, `is_locked`), `config::AppConfig` | **Implemented** | Scoped by email (not `(email, ip)`) to prevent IP rotation. Checked **before** password verification to avoid timing leaks. Successful login clears the counter immediately. Returns `AppError::AccountLocked` → HTTP 429. |
73	| Session management | `application::session` (`create_session`, `find_active_by_token`, `revoke_session`, `revoke_all_for_user`), `api::guards::AuthedPrincipal` | **Implemented** | 256-bit random tokens, SHA-256 hashed at rest, `expires_at` from `JWT_EXPIRATION_HOURS`, `revoked_at` set on logout or password change. Multiple concurrent sessions per user allowed. |
74	| Audit logging (immutable, SHA-256 hash chain) | `application::audit_service` (`record`, `compute_hash`, `verify_chain`, `search`, `ChainStatus`, `AuditSearch`), `api::audit_logs` | **Implemented** | `record` inserts into `audit_logs` and `audit_hash_chain` inside a single transaction with `SELECT ... FOR UPDATE` on the tip. Hash = `SHA-256(previous_hash \|\| '\|' \|\| sequence_be_i64 \|\| '\|' \|\| audit_log_id \|\| '\|' \|\| action \|\| '\|' \|\| payload)`. `verify_chain` walks in sequence order and reports the first break. `GET /api/v1/audit-logs` and `GET /api/v1/audit-logs/verify-chain` are admin-only. |
75	| Data masking foundation | `application::masking` (`mask_email_for_audit`, `mask_ip_for_audit`, `can_see_student_identifier`, `can_see_instructor_notes`) | Partial (Implemented) | Audit email and IP masking applied today by `api::audit_logs` for non-admin viewers (email → `user:<8-hex-prefix>`, IP → `[REDACTED]`). Student identifier and instructor-notes helpers are in place for Phase 3 domain code to consume. No masking for user.phone / user.ip_address fields yet. |
76	| Admin configuration primitives | `api::admin_config` (list/get/update), `backend/migrations/011_create_admin_settings.sql`, `backend/seeds/005_seed_admin_settings.sql` | **Implemented** | `admin_settings` key/value (JSON) store. All endpoints are `AdminOnly` and audited (`admin.config.read`, `admin.config.write` with old/new value diff). Seeded keys: `network.approved_ssids`, `checkin.duplicate_window_minutes=10`, `retention.audit_log_days=2555`, `retention.operational_events_days=1095`, `attachments.max_bytes`, `attachments.allowed_mime_types`, `auth.max_failed_logins=5`, `auth.lockout_minutes=15`, `auth.min_password_length=12`, `reports.schedules`. |
77	| Standardized error envelope | `errors::AppError`, `errors::ErrorEnvelope`, `errors::ErrorCode` | **Implemented** | Every failure emits `{ error: { code, message, fields?, request_id } }`. `code` is snake_case. `Internal` and `Database` details never leak to clients; they are only logged server-side by the `Responder` impl. `request_id` is populated by the `RequestId` guard so envelopes correlate with log lines. |
78	| Retention policies | `api::retention`, `domain::retention`, `application::retention_service`, `infrastructure::repositories::retention_repo` | Scaffolded, Schema Ready, Domain Modeled, Service Stub | Routes: list, create, update, execute. `retention_policies` table with `RetentionAction` enum: Delete, Anonymize, Archive, FlagForReview. Seeded defaults: audit_logs 365 days archive, sessions 30 days delete. Scheduled execution pending. |
79	| Data-at-rest encryption | -- | Not Started | MySQL TDE and attachment encryption are operator-provided. No AES-256-GCM wrapper around `LocalAttachmentStorage` yet. |
80	| SQL injection prevention | `infrastructure::repositories::*`, application services | Implemented (by construction) | All queries use parameterized `sqlx::query(...).bind(...)` or the `query!()` macros. No raw string concatenation anywhere in the Phase 2 code paths. |

---

## Phase 7 — what was done

### A. Route completeness

|---|---|

### B. Frontend UX
| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `frontend/src/pages/admin_settings.rs` | Full page: loads settings, inline edit, save with status banner |
| `frontend/src/pages/audit_logs.rs` | Full page: paginated table, action/date filters, chain verify button |

### C. Tests

- `report_service.rs` — 5 new unit tests (cron parsing, format DB round-trip)

- `README.md` — updated to Phase 7 with feature bullets

Perform a repository-wide hardening pass specifically for strict static delivery review. You may modify code, tests, docs, configuration, and file structure in this pass.

Your job is not to add speculative features. Your job is to make the delivered repository resistant to reviewer rejection.

Review the entire repository against the original project task and aggressively fix any static-review weakness in the following categories:

1. Prompt-to-code alignment
- find any task requirement not clearly implemented
- either implement it properly now or document the exact truthful limitation in README/docs if the requirement is constrained by platform reality
- do not leave any silent simplifications

2. Unauthorized simplifications
Search for and eliminate weak implementations such as:
- fake success responses
- mocked business results in production code
- placeholder report generation
- frontend-only authorization
- versioning implemented as overwrite-in-place
- dashboard values hardcoded instead of computed
- dry-run that partially commits
- “secure deletion” that is only a comment
- encryption wrappers that do nothing

3. Authentication and authorization
Review and harden:
- auth entry points
- route-level authorization
- object-level authorization
- function-level authorization
- department/user isolation
- admin-only route protection
- auditor read-only restrictions
- attachment access control
- report/export scope enforcement
- dashboard masking at API layer

4. Validation and error handling
Ensure all endpoints and critical service functions have explicit validation for:
- body
- query
- path params
- file uploads
- pagination/filter inputs
- boundary conditions

Standardize all error responses and ensure 401/403/404/409/422-style behaviors are consistent where applicable.

5. Logging and audit quality
Ensure logs are meaningful and structured for:
- auth events
- state changes
- permission/config changes
- imports/exports
- report generation/download
- check-in attempts/results
- critical failures

Ensure secrets and sensitive fields are not logged.
Ensure audit hash chaining remains intact and reviewable.

6. Tests and risk coverage
Close all important coverage gaps.
Strengthen both:
- unit_tests/
- API_tests/

Explicitly ensure coverage exists for:
- happy path
- validation failures
- unauthenticated access
- unauthorized access
- not found
- duplicate/conflict submission
- object-level authorization
- department/user isolation
- pagination/filtering/sorting where applicable
- edge/boundary conditions
- lockout logic
- version history integrity
- attachment validation/checksum
- import dry-run no-commit guarantee
- export/report scope restriction
- duplicate check-in and single retry only
- masked responses
- metric definition dependency flagging
- retention/deletion behavior

7. README / docs / code / Docker consistency
Verify that:
- README startup steps exactly match `docker-compose.yml`
- ports listed in docs match actual configuration
- seeded credentials in README match seed code
- migration/seed automation is truthfully documented
- folder structure in docs matches repo
- implemented features are not overstated
- requirement traceability is current

8. Docker completeness
Ensure:
- `docker compose up` is the one-command startup path
- all runtime dependencies are in Compose
- no undeclared host dependency exists
- ports are explicit
- migrations/seeds are automated
- no manual pre-start steps remain
- storage volumes are explicit
- environment config is clear and self-contained

9. Repository hygiene
Remove:
- junk files
- dead code
- stale scripts
- giant commented-out blocks
- noisy prints
- unused sample assets
- dependency pollution
- absolute local paths
- secrets
- private/internal references

Ensure `.gitignore` and `.dockerignore` are correct.

10. UI polish and reviewability
Across the Dioxus app, ensure major screens have:
- coherent layout
- responsive behavior
- clear visual hierarchy
- save/loading/error/empty/disabled states
- role-aware action presentation
- no obvious placeholder styling

11. Final static-evidence upgrades
Make the repository easy to review without running:
- add or improve diagrams/tables/docs where they materially help
- ensure key policies are explicit in code and docs
- ensure requirement traceability points to concrete files/modules/tests
- ensure important workflows are easy to follow from route -> service -> repository -> test -> UI

Deliverables for this pass:
- repository updated and hardened
- README updated
- docs/requirement_traceability.md fully refreshed
- docs/hardening_summary.md summarizing all significant fixes made in this pass

Do not produce a self-audit report in this pass. This pass is for fixing the repository, not just reviewing it.

{"description": "Deep audit of backend authorization, validation, and auth gaps", "subagent_type": "Explore", "prompt": "Perform a deep security and correctness audit of the Rust backend at /home/nahom/Desktop/Others/EaglePointAI/vibe-coding-projects/week-2/Scholarly/repo/backend/src.

I need specific findings with file:line references. Please:

1. Read `api/guards.rs` — report exactly what AuthedPrincipal and AdminOnly check. Does AdminOnly use a Capability check or just role check?

2. Read `application/authorization.rs` (full file) — report the complete role_allows matrix. Look for any capabilities that appear in Capability enum but are not handled in role_allows.

3. Read `api/sections.rs` — check if object-level authorization (ensuring the section belongs to caller's department or course) is enforced. Look for `ensure_section_in_scope` or similar calls.

4. Read `api/journals.rs` and `api/teaching_resources.rs` — check if update/delete routes verify ownership or department scope before mutating.

5. Read `api/courses.rs` — check the import endpoint: does it verify department scope before processing the CSV? Does the export endpoint enforce department scope?

6. Read `application/checkin_service.rs` — check if the duplicate window check uses the configured admin setting (not a hardcoded value). Does it read from admin_settings table or use a constant?

7. Read `application/dashboard_service.rs` — is every dashboard value computed from real data? Look for hardcoded numbers, `vec![]` returns, or `todo!()`.

8. Read `application/import_service.rs` — does dry_run actually avoid committing? Is there a transaction that is explicitly rolled back or not committed in dry-run mode?

9. Check `application/retention_service.rs` execute_policy function — does the `dry_run=true` path actually skip DB mutations? Look for branches on dry_run flag.

10. Look for any `println!`, `dbg!`, `eprintln!` in production code (not tests). Report file:line.

11. Read `api/reports.rs` — check that `download_artifact` verifies the run belongs to a report the caller can access (not just that the run_id exists).

12. Read `application/session.rs` — check if `find_active_by_token` properly validates that `revoked_at IS NULL` and `expires_at > NOW()`.

Report findings as a bulleted list with file:line references. Be specific about what you find — both gaps and confirmations of correct behavior."}

text.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information needed. Let me create my comprehensive security and completeness audit report.

## Security and Completeness Audit Report: Scholarly Dioxus Frontend

### 1. ROUTES & AUTHENTICATION GUARD

**Finding: CRITICAL - No route-level auth guards; auth only enforced in MainLayout**

- `/frontend/src/router.rs`: Routes 39-112 — All authenticated routes (lines 48-111) use `#[layout(MainLayout)]` decorator, which provides centralized auth check via `use_effect` at `main_layout.rs:142-146`
- The router itself contains no guard trait or permission check
- **Risk**: If MainLayout's `use_effect` is somehow bypassed or doesn't fire on first render, unauthenticated access to protected routes becomes possible
- **Mitigation confirmation**: The `MainLayout` component does redirect to `/login` if `!auth_state.read().is_authenticated()` (line 144) and renders empty div placeholder (line 151), so the pattern is defensive

### 2. LOGIN PAGE - INPUT VALIDATION & ERROR HANDLING

**Finding: PASS - Validation and error states present**

- `/frontend/src/pages/login.rs:17-25` — `is_valid_email()` helper validates email format (single `@`, non-empty local/domain, domain contains `.`)
- Lines 48-57: Email validation occurs before submission; password length checked (min 12 chars at line 52)
- Lines 90-91: Error banner renders conditionally (`if let Some(msg) = error()`)
- Lines 123-124: Submit button disabled during submission (`disabled: submitting()`)
- **Status**: Both client-side validation and error display working correctly

### 3. IMPORTS PAGE - DRY-RUN GATE & CONFIRMATION

**Finding: PASS - Dry-run is required before live commit**

- `/frontend/src/pages/imports.rs:48-53` — `caller_can_import()` checks role at component level (Admin or DepartmentHead)
- Lines 156-165: Tracks `courses_dry_ok` and `sections_dry_ok` signals — toggled to `true` only after successful dry-run (lines 267-268)
- Lines 180-182: Commit radio button is conditionally disabled when `!can_commit_now`
- Lines 440-445: Commit button disabled unless both `current_mode == Mode::Commit && can_commit_now`
- Lines 245-249: Failing dry-run **clears** prior dry-run approval by setting flags to false
- **Status**: Dry-run validation gate is correctly implemented; live commit cannot proceed without successful dry-run

### 4. JOURNAL LIST & JOURNAL DETAIL - ROLE-BASED VISIBILITY

**Finding: PASS - Approve/Publish buttons hidden from non-authorized roles**

- `/frontend/src/pages/journal_list.rs:17-26` — `caller_can_author()` checks for Admin OR Librarian
- Line 47: `can_author` computed once per render
- Line 151-160: "New journal" button only rendered if `can_author` is true

- `/frontend/src/pages/journal_detail.rs:19-28` — Same `caller_can_author()` check (Admin OR Librarian)
- Lines 329-330: Button visibility controlled by `can_approve = can_author && effective_state == "draft"` and `can_publish = can_author && effective_state == "approved"`
- Lines 353-370: Approve/Publish buttons rendered conditionally inside `if can_approve` and `if can_publish` blocks
- **Status**: Role checks are in place; buttons are hidden, not just disabled. Backend will still enforce on API side.

### 5. COURSE LIST & COURSE DETAIL - ROLE-BASED VISIBILITY

**Finding: PASS - Role checks for course management**

- `/frontend/src/pages/course_list.rs:18-27` — `caller_can_author()` checks Admin OR DepartmentHead
- Line 52: Computed once; "New course" button shown only if `can_author` (line 216-226)
- `/frontend/src/pages/course_detail.rs:22-31` — Same pattern: Admin OR DepartmentHead

4. Errors and Fixes:

- **Session revocation wrong column name**: `users.rs` initially used `UPDATE user_sessions SET revoked = 1` but the schema has `revoked_at DATETIME DEFAULT NULL`. Fixed by reading `application/session.rs` which showed the correct pattern: `UPDATE user_sessions SET revoked_at = NOW() WHERE user_id = ? AND revoked_at IS NULL`

- **Wrong function name in admin_settings.rs**: Used `use_api_client()` which doesn't exist. The hook is `use_api()` in `hooks/use_api.rs`. Fixed by removing the unused import and not calling the function.

- **Write on unread file error** (from previous session): Attempting to write files without reading them first. Fixed by always reading before writing.

5. Problem Solving:

**Report download scope gap (identified but not yet fixed):**
The `download_artifact()` function in `report_service.rs` checks `Capability::ReportRead` but does not enforce department scope. The `find_by_id` function in `report_repo.rs` takes no scope parameter. This means a DepartmentHead in Department A who knows a report's `run_id` from Department B could download that artifact. Fix needed: after loading `report_row`, apply `content_scope(principal)` check against the report's `created_by` user's department.

**Resource instructor ownership (needs investigation):**
The resource service grants `ResourceWrite` to both Librarians and Instructors. The capability matrix documents "* instructors can write resources they own." However, `create_draft_version` only checks `require(principal, Capability::ResourceWrite)?` without verifying `principal.user_id == resource.owner_id` for instructors. Librarians may be intended to edit all resources; instructors only their own.

171	        .transpose()?;
172

182	        std::path::Path::new(&full_path).exists()
183	    } else {
184	        false
185	    };
186	
187	    Ok(ReportRunView {
188	        id,
189	        report_id,
190	        report_title,
191	        triggered_by,
192	        triggered_source,

62	/// List all reports visible under the given scope.
63	/// Scope is based on the report's `created_by` user's department (joined via users table).
64	/// For ScopeFilter::All, every report is returned.
65	/// For department scopes, only reports created by users in the same department are returned.
66	pub async fn find_all(pool: &MySqlPool, scope: &ScopeFilter) -> AppResult<Vec<ReportRow>> {
67	    let mut sql = String::from(
68	        r#"
69	        SELECT r.id, r.title, r.description,
70	               CAST(r.query_definition AS CHAR) AS query_definition,
71	               r.default_format, r.created_by,
72	               r.created_at, r.updated_at
73	          FROM reports r
74	          LEFT JOIN users u ON r.created_by = u.id
75	         WHERE 1=1
76	        "#,
77	    );
78	
79	    let dept_id: Option<Uuid> = match scope {
80	        ScopeFilter::All => None,
81	        ScopeFilter::Department(d) => Some(*d),
82	        ScopeFilter::DepartmentOrOwned { department_id, .. } => Some(*department_id),
83	        ScopeFilter::OwnedBy(_) => None,
84	        ScopeFilter::None => {
85	            // Return empty set immediately — no rows visible.
86	            return Ok(Vec::new());
87	        }
88	    };
89	
90	    let owner_id: Option<Uuid> = match scope {
91	        ScopeFilter::OwnedBy(uid) => Some(*uid),
92	        ScopeFilter::DepartmentOrOwned { owner_id, .. } => Some(*owner_id),
93	        _ => None,
94	    };
95	
96	    // Build WHERE clause
97	    match scope {
98	        ScopeFilter::All => {}
99	        ScopeFilter::Department(_) => {
100	            sql.push_str(" AND u.department_id = ?");

358	            WHERE id = ?"#,
359	    )

361	    .bind(version_id.to_string())
362	    .execute(pool)
363	    .await
